<!-- GENERATED by sphinxpress. Do not edit by hand. -->

<section id="sphinxpress">
<h1>sphinxpress</h1>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span></code> publishes multiple Sphinx documentation projects as one documentation product:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Jekyll/GitHub Pages pages generated from Sphinx JSON output</p></li>
<li><p>a combined EPUB book</p></li>
<li><p>a combined PDF book</p></li>
</ul>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span></code> does not replace Sphinx. Each source project remains a normal Sphinx project with its own <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">conf.py</span></code>, root document, extensions, and source files. <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span></code> orchestrates those projects and writes deterministic generated output for a site or book pipeline.</p>
<section id="when-to-use-it">
<h2>When to use it</h2>
<p>Use <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span></code> when you maintain several related Python tools or libraries and want one documentation site and one combined book artifact without merging the original docs projects.</p>
</section>
<section id="first-steps">
<h2>First steps</h2>
<ol class="arabic simple">
<li><p>Install the package in your docs build environment.</p></li>
<li><p>Create <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress.toml</span></code> at the repository root.</p></li>
<li><p>Add one <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[[projects]]</span></code> entry for each Sphinx docs project.</p></li>
<li><p>Run <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span> <span class="pre">check</span></code>.</p></li>
<li><p>Run <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span> <span class="pre">build-site</span> <span class="pre">--all</span></code>.</p></li>
<li><p>Commit or publish the generated Jekyll pages according to your site workflow.</p></li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>python<span class="w"> </span>-m<span class="w"> </span>pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>sphinxpress
sphinxpress<span class="w"> </span>check
sphinxpress<span class="w"> </span>build-site<span class="w"> </span>--all
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>For development:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>python<span class="w"> </span>-m<span class="w"> </span>pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>-e<span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;.[dev,docs]&quot;</span>
python<span class="w"> </span>-m<span class="w"> </span>pytest<span class="w"> </span>-q
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
